preamble: The shift from the voiceless retroflex affricate /ʈʂ/ to the voiced postalveolar affricate /dʒ/ is a documented phonetic transformation, primarily observed as a lenition process involving both voicing and a slight fronting of the place of articulation.
phoneticEffects: This transformation involves two primary phonetic changes: voicing, where the voiceless affricate becomes voiced, and de-retroflexion (or fronting), where the point of articulation shifts from retroflex to postalveolar

examples.note: In Taiwanese Mandarin, the voiceless retroflex affricate /ʈʂ/ (represented by 'zh-' in Pinyin) can be realized as a voiced postalveolar affricate /dʒ/.
